title: Trump Faces the Complicated Reality of a Costly, Unpopular War in Iran
summary: President Trump’s predictions of a relatively short-term conflict with minimal economic consequences appear to be crumbling.

title: What to Know About the Mideast Standoff
summary: Negotiations to end the war are at an impasse over Iran’s nuclear program and the Strait of Hormuz, which remains mostly shut.

title: Jordanians Struggle as Mideast Wars Scare Tourists Away
summary: Though Jordan mostly sat out the conflicts, its tourism high season was nearly wiped out at popular sites. Visitors canceled flights, hotels and tours.

title: Behind Supreme Court Voting Rights Ruling, a Clash Over the Reality of Racism
summary: The Supreme Court ruling said there must be proof that a racial group was “intentionally” disadvantaged. The dissent called it “well-nigh impossible.”

title: Indiana Primary Election Will Test Trump’s Influence With Republicans
summary: The election on Tuesday will test the influence of President Trump, who endorsed challengers to Republican state senators who scuttled his push to redraw political maps.

title: One Issue Uniting Democrats and Republicans? Worries About A.I.
summary: The growing unease over artificial intelligence is something elements of the left and the right can agree on in a polarized age.

title: MAHA Awaits Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s Definition of Ultraprocessed Foods
summary: Some yogurts and peanut butters could be considered ultraprocessed as scientists and MAHA supporters push for a strict definition.

title: Tariffs, Rebates, Chaos: Boutique Businesses Wonder What’s Next
summary: A Brooklyn general store that sells Taiwanese imports was pummeled by tariffs. A rebate would help, but the confusion still lingers.

title: The Last Days of Butter Ridge
summary: The Watsons were dairy farmers for generations, the rhythms of their lives defined by their cows. Until this spring.

title: An Insurgency Threatens U.S. Mining Ambitions in Pakistan
summary: Attacks by the Baloch Liberation Army could derail Pakistan’s plans for a billion-dollar mining deal with the Trump administration.

title: A Perilous Train Ride Home in Pakistan on the Jaffer Express
summary: We witnessed crossfire while waiting for the Jaffer Express, a vital lifeline through Balochistan. For many, it’s the only way home.

title: Kathryn Stockett Has Finally Followed Up ‘The Help’
summary: It was a blockbuster hit, yet she says she was “fired” by her publisher. After a spell in Bali, she’s back on home turf with “The Calamity Club.”

title: Why the A.I. Job Apocalypse (Probably) Won’t Happen
summary: The feeling of efficiency should be mistrusted.

title: In a Minneapolis Public School, a Cure for Toxic Politics
summary: This school gave me hope for our democracy.

title: Bruce vs. Donald
summary: We look at Bruce Springsteen’s political activism.

title: Fear and Vigilance Are Now Constant Companions for Many American Jews
summary: Each new attack, like the London stabbings last week, brings a heightened sense of caution to an already wary population.

title: The U.S. Set Off a Corruption Scandal That Is Gripping Mexico
summary: The U.S. indictment of a Mexican governor has spotlighted cartel corruption, strained cross-border relations and handed President Claudia Sheinbaum a thorny choice.

title: The New Schoolyard Fight: Shrinking Enrollments and Too Many Classrooms
summary: A battle in one New York neighborhood echoes struggles unfolding in school systems across the nation that are experiencing a decline in students.
